proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Patients flagged as unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response rate than fit patients.
Secondary AML (secondary_aml=1) is associated with lower objective_response than de novo AML.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate decreases monotonically with ECOG: 0.231 (ECOG 0, n=17592), 0.174 (ECOG 1, n=24971), 0.129 (ECOG 2, n=7437). Logit beta vs ECOG 0: -0.353 (p<1e-46) for ECOG 1, -0.706 (p<1e-72) for ECOG 2.

Counter-intuitively, unfit_for_intensive=1 has HIGHER response (0.221, n=20056) than unfit=0 (0.165, n=29944), diff +0.056, p<1e-30. Suggests confounding by treatment assignment (e.g., ven/aza enriched in unfit cohort).
Response rate 0.188 in secondary_aml=1 vs 0.187 in secondary_aml=0, diff +0.001, p=0.84. No measurable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have higher objective_response rate than npm1_mutation=0 patients.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have lower objective_response rate than tp53_mutation=0 patients.
Complex karyotype (complex_karyotype=1) is associated with lower objective_response.
FLT3-ITD (flt3_itd=1) is associated with a different objective_response rate than flt3_itd=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response 0.235 in npm1+ (n=15014) vs 0.167 in npm1- (n=34986), diff +0.067, p<1e-50. Strong favorable main effect.
Response 0.166 in tp53+ (n=4985) vs 0.190 in tp53- (n=45015), diff -0.024, p=3.2e-05. Confirmed unfavorable main effect.
Response 0.168 in CK+ (n=7462) vs 0.191 in CK- (n=42538), diff -0.023, p=3.1e-06. Confirmed unfavorable main effect.
Response 0.189 in FLT3-ITD+ (n=10000) vs 0.187 in FLT3-ITD- (n=40000), diff +0.002, p=0.63. No main-effect signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher marrow blast percentage (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher white blood cell count (wbc_k_per_ul) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logit beta for age_years = -0.0002, p=0.88. Response is essentially flat across age deciles (range 0.178-0.199). No detectable age main effect.
Logit beta = -0.0049 per 1% blasts, p=2.4e-16. Confirmed unfavorable main effect.
Logit beta = -0.0018 per 1k/uL, p=1.9e-04. Confirmed unfavorable main effect.
Logit beta = +0.118 per g/dL albumin, p=2.9e-07. Confirmed favorable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher C-reactive protein (crp_mg_l) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with lower objective_response.
Routine chemistry panel variables (creatinine_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl) are not associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta = -0.036 per 1% weight loss, p=1.1e-30. Strong unfavorable main effect.
Logit beta = -0.0064 per mg/L, p=6.0e-06. Confirmed unfavorable main effect.
Logit beta = -1e-04 per U/L, p=0.35. No main-effect signal univariately.
Logit beta = -6e-04, p=0.92. No main-effect signal.

analyses.code: univariate logit for each chem panel variable
analyses.result_summary: creatinine p=0.72, sodium p=0.62, potassium p=0.76, calcium p=0.81, BUN p=0.55, bilirubin p=0.22, AST p=0.13, ALT p=0.20, alk phos p=0.05. No clinically meaningful main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with venetoclax+azacitidine (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1) is associated with higher objective_response than not receiving it.
Treatment with 7+3 induction (treatment_7plus3=1) is associated with higher objective_response than not receiving it.
Each targeted therapy (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) has a positive main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response 0.220 on ven/aza (n=20020) vs 0.166 off (n=29980), diff +0.054, p<1e-30. Strong favorable main effect.
Response 0.189 on 7+3 (n=22580) vs 0.186 off (n=27420), diff +0.003, p=0.46. No detectable main effect overall.

Midostaurin: 0.186 vs 0.188 (p=0.66). Gilteritinib: 0.181 vs 0.189 (p=0.13). Ivosidenib: 0.188 vs 0.187 (p=0.91). Enasidenib: 0.186 vs 0.188 (p=0.78). None has a positive main effect; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response include ecog_ps (negative), unfit_for_intensive (positive), npm1_mutation (positive), tp53_mutation (negative), complex_karyotype (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), wbc_k_per_ul (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), crp_mg_l (negative), and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit confirms (all p<0.001 unless noted): ecog_ps -0.36, unfit_for_intensive +0.37, npm1 +0.44, tp53 -0.19, complex_karyotype -0.17, blast_pct_marrow -0.005, wbc -0.002, albumin +0.12, weight_loss -0.037, crp -0.007, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine +0.36. Pseudo-R2 0.032.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Midostaurin's effect on objective_response is more favorable in flt3_itd=1 patients than in flt3_itd=0 patients (clinically expected).
Gilteritinib's effect on objective_response is more favorable in flt3_itd=1 than in flt3_itd=0.
Ivosidenib's effect on objective_response is more favorable in idh1_mutation=1 than in idh1_mutation=0.
Enasidenib's effect on objective_response is more favorable in idh2_mutation=1 than in idh2_mutation=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta -0.106, p=0.20. In FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin 0.176 vs 0.191 (diff -0.015). In ITD-: 0.188 vs 0.187 (diff +0.001). DiD -0.016 - no support for biomarker-targeted benefit; if anything midostaurin trends slightly worse in ITD+.
Interaction beta +0.079, p=0.32. In ITD+: gilt 0.191 vs 0.189 (diff +0.002). In ITD-: 0.179 vs 0.189 (diff -0.010). No significant heterogeneity.
Interaction beta -0.30, p=0.077. In IDH1+: ivo 0.154 vs 0.194 (diff -0.040). In IDH1-: 0.191 vs 0.187 (diff +0.004). Trend opposite to clinical expectation; not significant.
Interaction beta +0.039, p=0.79. In IDH2+: ena 0.195 vs 0.191 (diff +0.004). In IDH2-: 0.185 vs 0.187 (diff -0.002). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of venetoclax+azacitidine on objective_response is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients (positive treatment-by-fitness interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: beta +0.883, p=1.1e-78. In unfit=1: ven +0.145 (0.308 vs 0.163). In unfit=0: ven -0.008 (0.160 vs 0.168). DiD +0.153. Ven/aza benefit is essentially confined to the unfit subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ven/aza-treated patients, tp53_mutation=1 abrogates the response benefit (negative ven/aza-by-tp53 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Negative interaction beta -0.41, p=4.7e-07. In tp53-: ven 0.226 vs 0.166, diff +0.060. In tp53+: ven 0.164 vs 0.167, diff -0.004. tp53+ patients derive no measurable ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ven/aza-treated patients, npm1_mutation=1 dramatically amplifies the response benefit (positive ven/aza-by-npm1 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Largest interaction in dataset: beta +1.05, p=5.5e-102. In npm1+: ven 0.347 vs 0.160, diff +0.187. In npm1-: 0.165 vs 0.169, diff -0.004. Almost the entire ven/aza main effect comes from npm1+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ven/aza response benefit is concentrated in patients who are BOTH npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1; in either-or-neither subgroups the benefit is small or absent.

analyses.code: stratified rates by npm1 x unfit x venaza
analyses.result_summary: Within npm1+/unfit+: ven 0.633 vs 0.160 (diff +0.474, n=2401 vs 3585). Within npm1+/unfit-: 0.157 vs 0.160 (diff -0.003). Within npm1-/unfit+: 0.169 vs 0.164 (diff +0.005). Within npm1-/unfit-: 0.162 vs 0.172 (diff -0.010). Both npm1=1 and unfit=1 are required.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among npm1+/unfit+ patients on ven/aza, tp53_mutation=1 still suppresses the response benefit; the ven/aza effect is therefore concentrated in npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- patients.

analyses.code: rates in npm1+ x unfit+ x tp53 x ven cells
analyses.result_summary: npm1+/unfit+/tp53-: ven 0.691 (n=2125) vs 0.157 (n=3224), diff +0.534. npm1+/unfit+/tp53+: ven 0.188 (n=276) vs 0.183 (n=361), diff +0.005. tp53+ wipes out the benefit even within the npm1+/unfit+ enriched subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- patients on ven/aza, complex_karyotype=1 also suppresses the response benefit; the ven/aza-responsive subgroup is npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- AND complex_karyotype-.

analyses.code: rates in npm1+ x unfit+ x ck x ven cells (within tp53-)
analyses.result_summary: npm1+/unfit+/CK-: ven 0.717 (n=2025) vs 0.161 (n=3052), diff +0.556. npm1+/unfit+/CK+: ven 0.184 (n=376) vs 0.152 (n=533), diff +0.031. CK+ also abrogates the ven/aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ven/aza-responsive subgroup is fully defined as npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; within this subgroup ven/aza dramatically increases objective_response, while outside it the effect is null.

analyses.code: create indicator; chi2 ven vs response within and outside subgroup; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (n=4531): ven 0.786 (n=1790) vs 0.158 (n=2741), diff +0.628 (chi2 p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=45469): ven 0.164 (n=18230) vs 0.167 (n=27239), diff -0.003 (chi2 p=0.42). Logit interaction (subgroup x ven) beta +2.99, p<1e-200.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates (npm1+, unfit+, tp53-, ck-) is necessary; relaxing any one of them substantially reduces the ven/aza response rate.

analyses.code: drop one predicate at a time; recompute ven=1 response rate
analyses.result_summary: Full subgroup ven=1 rate 0.786. Drop tp53- -> 0.717. Drop CK- -> 0.691. Drop unfit+ -> 0.403 (largest drop, because in fit patients ven has no benefit). Drop npm1+ -> 0.349 (also large). All four predicates contribute; the interaction is genuinely four-way, with npm1+ and unfit+ being the most necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, the ven/aza effect does not depend further on age, sex, ECOG, blasts, WBC, albumin, LDH, CRP, FLT3, IDH1/2, or secondary AML status (no remaining heterogeneity).

analyses.code: logit ven*moderator within subgroup for each moderator
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values within subgroup: age 0.17, ECOG 0.09, sex 0.29, WBC 0.68, blasts 0.25, albumin 0.83, LDH 0.46, CRP 0.49, FLT3-ITD 0.22, FLT3-TKD 0.48, IDH1 0.31, IDH2 0.026 (only nominally; would not survive correction), secondary AML 0.94. No clinically convincing further moderators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, no other treatment (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) shows a measurable benefit on objective_response.

analyses.code: rates by treatment within complement of subgroup; chi2 tests
analyses.result_summary: Within complement (n=45469): midostaurin 0.165 vs 0.166 (p=0.80); gilteritinib 0.158 vs 0.167 (p=0.08); ivosidenib 0.170 vs 0.165 (p=0.51); enasidenib 0.159 vs 0.166 (p=0.24); 7+3 0.167 vs 0.164 (p=0.45). No alternative treatment helps.

proposed_hypotheses.text: 7+3 induction has a positive effect on objective_response specifically in fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients.
Within the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, adding 7+3 does not further increase objective_response beyond ven/aza alone.

analyses.result_summary: Unfit=0: 7+3 0.167 vs 0.163 (diff +0.004). Unfit=1: 7+3 0.221 vs 0.221 (diff 0.000). 7+3 has no detectable benefit even within fit patients.

analyses.code: stratified rates within npm1+/unfit+/tp53-/CK- by ven and 7+3
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: ven=1/7+3=0 0.793, ven=1/7+3=1 0.778, ven=0/7+3=0 0.161, ven=0/7+3=1 0.156. The benefit comes entirely from ven/aza; 7+3 adds nothing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous biomarkers (age, WBC, blasts, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin) do not meaningfully modify the ven/aza treatment effect after the four-predicate subgroup is accounted for.

analyses.code: logit ven*continuous in full data; check residual interaction in subgroup
analyses.result_summary: In full data, ven*blast_pct_marrow p=0.0035 and ven*weight_loss p=0.039 (positive interactions: at higher blasts, ven trends more favorable). Within the four-predicate subgroup, all continuous interaction p>0.17. Continuous moderation is largely a side-effect of the subgroup, not residual after conditioning.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive small-multifeature subgroup search of treatment-effect heterogeneity across all six treatments will not reveal another subgroup with a treatment-response benefit comparable to the ven/aza subgroup.

analyses.code: treatment x binary feature interaction screen sorted by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions: ven*npm1 p=5.5e-102; ven*unfit p=1.1e-78; ven*tp53 p=4.7e-07; ven*CK p=6.0e-06; enasidenib*sex p=2.2e-04 (DiD +0.048: in females, ena 0.212 vs 0.187, in males 0.165 vs 0.188). All other treatment-by-binary interactions p>0.001. Only ven/aza shows large-magnitude heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Enasidenib has a sex-dependent effect: in sex_female=1 patients, enasidenib increases objective_response, while in male patients it does not (or trends downward).

analyses.code: rates by sex_female x treatment_enasidenib; further stratification by IDH2
analyses.result_summary: Females: ena 0.212 (n=1781) vs 0.187 (n=20590), diff +0.025. Males: ena 0.165 (n=2191) vs 0.188 (n=25438), diff -0.023. Interaction p=2.2e-04. Within IDH2+ females: ena 0.241 (n=166) vs 0.200 (n=1959), diff +0.041 - directionally consistent but small absolute n. Effect is real but modest in absolute terms (~+2-4%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four-predicate ven/aza subgroup (npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- AND CK-) survives multivariable adjustment for all baseline covariates; the interaction is not explained by confounding with other prognostic features.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ subgroup*ven + ecog + age + albumin + blast + crp + weight_loss + ...', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After multivariable adjustment for ecog_ps, age, sex, blast %, WBC, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and other treatments, the subgroup x ven/aza interaction beta remains ~+3.0 with p<1e-200. The four-predicate definition captures essentially all ven/aza heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, npm1_mutation=1 is still associated with higher response, but the absolute increment is much smaller (favorable prognostic main effect that is amplified by ven/aza).

analyses.code: rates by npm1 within ven=0 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Among ven=0 patients: npm1+ 0.160 (n=8989) vs npm1- 0.169 (n=20991), diff -0.009 (p>0.05). Without ven/aza, npm1 confers no benefit on response. The npm1 main effect observed earlier is driven entirely by the ven/aza-treated, npm1+ subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation=1 has an unfavorable effect on response that is independent of treatment (true prognostic), whereas the npm1 favorable effect is treatment-dependent (predictive of ven/aza response).

analyses.code: rates by tp53 within ven=0 stratum and within ven=1 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Among ven=0: tp53+ 0.169 (n=3300) vs tp53- 0.166 (n=26680), diff +0.003 (p>0.05) - tp53 prognostic effect is small/null without ven/aza. Among ven=1: tp53+ 0.164 (n=1685) vs tp53- 0.226 (n=18335), diff -0.062 (p<1e-10). The 'tp53 unfavorable' main effect in the full cohort comes mainly from tp53+ patients failing to derive ven/aza benefit. tp53 is predictive of LOSS of ven/aza response, not strongly prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: Treatment with venetoclax+azacitidine (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1) increases objective_response substantially (absolute risk increase ~+0.63, ven=1 rate 0.79 vs ven=0 rate 0.16) ONLY in patients meeting all four predicates: npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0. In all other patients ven/aza has no measurable effect on objective_response. No comparable treatment-effect subgroup exists for any other treatment in the dataset.

analyses.result_summary: Final test confirms: subgroup x ven/aza interaction beta +2.99 (p<1e-200). In subgroup, ven/aza ven=1 rate 0.786 vs ven=0 rate 0.158 (diff +0.628). Outside subgroup, ven=1 rate 0.164 vs ven=0 rate 0.167 (diff -0.003, NS). The four-predicate definition is the complete subgroup; tp53=0 and complex_karyotype=0 are necessary 'unfavorable-value-suppression' predicates that must accompany npm1=1 and unfit_for_intensive=1.
